title: Java Stream API - Transforming elements
question: How to use Stream.map() in Java?
answer:
The map() method transforms each element of a stream by applying a function to it.

**Syntax:**
```java
<R> Stream<R> map(Function<? super T, ? extends R> mapper)
```

**Example:**
```java
List<String> names = Arrays.asList("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie");

// Convert to uppercase
List<String> upperNames = names.stream()
    .map(String::toUpperCase)
    .collect(Collectors.toList());
// Result: ["ALICE", "BOB", "CHARLIE"]

// Map to lengths
List<Integer> lengths = names.stream()
    .map(String::length)
    .collect(Collectors.toList());
// Result: [5, 3, 7]
```

**Use cases:**
- Transforming object properties
- Type conversion
- Extracting specific fields from objects
- Applying calculations to each element
